title: Additional cancer-type worked-example case studies
context: After the three core courses are stable, additional cancer-type case studies can deepen the curriculum with more examples. Candidate case studies are chosen for pedagogical power: a cancer with documented overdiagnosis from screen-detection (e.g., thyroid or prostate cancer) to illustrate overdiagnosis concretely; a cancer with a real mortality-reducing screen to illustrate what genuine screening benefit looks like; and a rare cancer for small-number and confidence-interval lessons. These remain HIGH riskTier because they are patient-facing content with cancer statistics. All three expert reviewers must sign off.
objective: Author additional cancer-type worked-example case studies selected for maximum pedagogical value: at minimum (1) a cancer with documented overdiagnosis from screen-detection to illustrate overdiagnosis concretely; (2) a cancer with a real mortality-reducing screen to illustrate what genuine screening benefit looks like vs. a survival artifact; (3) a rare cancer for small-number instability and confidence-interval lessons. All cases must be built from cited aggregate data and meet all editorial guardrail requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: At least three cancer-type case studies authored, selected for pedagogical power: an overdiagnosed screen-detected cancer, a cancer with demonstrated mortality-reducing screening, and a rare cancer for small-number/CI lessons
Each case study is built from cited aggregate data (no individual-level data) with data vintage and provenance register entries
Each case study names the misconception(s) it targets and demonstrates the correct interpretation step by step
HIGH-tier framing: persistent 'Education only — not medical advice' frame; no individualized prognosis or screening recommendation
Biostatistician/epidemiologist sign-off recorded for all case studies in the reviewers ledger
Oncologist sign-off recorded confirming clinical accuracy and safe framing for all case studies
Patient-advocate sign-off recorded confirming plain-language clarity and harm-avoidance
output: A set of additional cancer-type worked-example case studies (at minimum overdiagnosed screen-detected, real mortality-reducing screen, and rare cancer for CI/small-number) — each built from cited aggregate data, pedagogically targeted, and signed off by all three expert reviewers.
